(2) Terminal gratuity is in addition to any other gratuity
or other benefit payable under any written law in respect of
the office to which the terminal gratuity relates.
(3) Where a person has been paid terminal gratuity, he
shall not be paid another terminal grstuity notwithstanding
that he has served for a further period in a specified office.
(4) Where a person who has held a spec:fied office is, upon
his ceasing to hold that office appointed, nominated or elected
•

hold the first mentioned-office held one or 1pore specified
offices for not less than the ten year period or the two full
parliamentary terms referred to in section foitr, be entitled to
be paid terminal gratuity based on the highest earnings of a
specified office held by him before the expiry of the said period
or terms notwithstanding that he continues to hold another
specified office.
